channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
item.description: The U.S. has fought three major wars in the past quarter-century, in Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. None has gone as planned.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. has fought three major wars in the past quarter-century, in Iran, Iraq and Afghanistan. None has gone as planned.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: In first Congressional testimony, new Fed Chief Warsh vows to fight inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee for the first time Wednesday since taking over as chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh pledged to bring inflation under control.
item.content:encoded: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee for the first time Wednesday since taking over as chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh pledged to bring inflation under control.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Iran's hardliners angry at talks with U.S.
item.description: The recent funeral for Iran's Supreme Leader drew attention to the many Iranians seeking revenge against the U.S. — and are angry at their government for entering ceasefire talks.
item.content:encoded: The recent funeral for Iran's Supreme Leader drew attention to the many Iranians seeking revenge against the U.S. — and are angry at their government for entering ceasefire talks.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: How to go from horseshoe hairline to baldmaxxing
item.description: Harry James helps men feel more comfortable embracing their baldness — he calls it "baldmaxxing."
item.content:encoded: Harry James helps men feel more comfortable embracing their baldness — he calls it "baldmaxxing."
item.dc:creator: Linah Mohammad

item.title: Fear and hope in Utah in the shadow of America's largest wildfire
item.description: People in Monticello, Utah have been tense and preparing to evacuate since shortly after the Babylon fire started June 26th
item.content:encoded:
People in Monticello, Utah have been tense and preparing to evacuate since shortly after the Babylon fire started June 26th

(Image credit: Inciweb)
item.dc:creator: Adam Burke

item.title: Old-school digicams are everywhere. Why?
item.description: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.content:encoded: People who weren't yet alive in the 1990s and early 2000s are buying up old-school point-and-shoot cameras. For some, it's a trendy retro vibe; for others, a rebellion against the smartphone era.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Sen. Shaheen discusses new Russia sanctions bill
item.description: A conversation with Jeanne Shaheen, Democratic Senator from New Hampshire, about a bipartisan bill she put together with the late Senator Lindsey Graham that slaps new sanctions on Russia.
item.content:encoded: A conversation with Jeanne Shaheen, Democratic Senator from New Hampshire, about a bipartisan bill she put together with the late Senator Lindsey Graham that slaps new sanctions on Russia.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Harland-Dunaway

item.title: Argentina is back in the World Cup final after a thrilling semifinal win over England
item.description: Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina survived another heart-stopping match when it scored two late goals to topple England and extend the Three Lions' six-decade-long wait for another World Cup trophy.

(Image credit: Shaun Botterill)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: The crews stuck at sea as the Strait of Hormuz crisis grows
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.
item.content:encoded: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: Behind Amelia Earhart's flight suit, a story of American fashion and celebrity
item.description: Amelia Earhart was famous as an early aviatrix. Her flight suit is part of a bigger story about the history of American fashion and celebrity.
item.content:encoded: Amelia Earhart was famous as an early aviatrix. Her flight suit is part of a bigger story about the history of American fashion and celebrity.
item.dc:creator: Clare Lombardo
